target.title: Add Antigravity agy CLI fallback
target.body:
## Summary
- add an Antigravity `agy` CLI HTTPS fallback when the desktop app is closed
- harden warm-session ownership so live CodexBar owners keep their persisted CLI records
- add focused coverage for CLI session lifecycle, fetch strategy fallback, and menu metric selection
- leave `CHANGELOG.md` untouched for the release flow

## Verification
- `swift test --filter AntigravityCLISessionTests`
- `make check`
- `swift test`

## Runtime proof: closed-desktop `agy` fallback

Local bundle built and launched from this branch with `./Scripts/compile_and_run.sh`.

Desktop Antigravity/language server was not running:

```text
$ pgrep -fl "/Applications/Antigravity|Google Antigravity|language_server"
# no output
```

To avoid OAuth token-account routing in my normal config, I used a temporary config with only Antigravity enabled and no token accounts:

```text
$ CODEXBAR_CONFIG=/tmp/codexbar-agy-proof.json ./CodexBar.app/Contents/Helpers/CodexBarCLI config enable --provider antigravity --format json --pretty
{
  "provider" : "antigravity",
  "displayName" : "Antigravity",
  "enabled" : true,
  "configPath" : "/tmp/codexbar-agy-proof.json"
}
```

Auto mode fell through to the CLI HTTPS fallback and returned quotas from `agy`:

```text
$ CODEXBAR_CONFIG=/tmp/codexbar-agy-proof.json ./CodexBar.app/Contents/Helpers/CodexBarCLI usage --provider antigravity --source auto --format json --pretty --log-level debug
2026-06-05T14:03:29+0800 debug ... [CodexBarCore] Antigravity CLI session started binary=agy pid=<redacted>
2026-06-05T14:03:29+0800 debug ... source=cli-https path=/exa.language_server_pb.LanguageServerService/GetUserStatus port=<redacted> ... Antigravity request/parse attempt failed
2026-06-05T14:03:32+0800 debug ... [CodexBarCore] Antigravity CLI session stopping pid=<redacted> reason=one-shot CLI fetch
[
  {
    "provider" : "antigravity",
    "source" : "cli",
    "usage" : {
      "accountEmail" : "<redacted>",
      "identity" : {
        "accountEmail" : "<redacted>",
        "loginMethod" : "Google AI Pro",
        "providerID" : "antigravity"
      },
      "primary" : { "usedPercent" : 100, "resetsAt" : "<redacted>" },
      "secondary" : { "usedPercent" : 0, "resetsAt" : "<redacted>" },
      "tertiary" : { "usedPercent" : 0, "resetsAt" : "<redacted>" },
      "extraRateWindows" : [
        { "title" : "Claude Opus 4.6 (Thinking)", "window" : { "usedPercent" : 100, "resetsAt" : "<redacted>" } },
        { "title" : "Claude Sonnet 4.6 (Thinking)", "window" : { "usedPercent" : 100, "resetsAt" : "<redacted>" } },
        { "title" : "Gemini 3.1 Pro (High)", "window" : { "usedPercent" : 0, "resetsAt" : "<redacted>" } }
      ],
      "updatedAt" : "<redacted>"
    }
  }
]
```

Explicit CLI source also returned `source: "cli"` and stopped the one-shot session after the fetch:

```text
$ CODEXBAR_CONFIG=/tmp/codexbar-agy-proof.json ./CodexBar.app/Contents/Helpers/CodexBarCLI usage --provider antigravity --source cli --format json --pretty --status --log-level debug
2026-06-05T14:03:12+0800 debug ... [CodexBarCore] Reaping stale Antigravity CLI session pid=<redacted>
2026-06-05T14:03:12+0800 debug ... [CodexBarCore] Antigravity CLI session started binary=agy pid=<redacted>
2026-06-05T14:03:15+0800 debug ... [CodexBarCore] Antigravity CLI session stopping pid=<redacted> reason=one-shot CLI fetch
[
  { "provider" : "antigravity", "source" : "cli", "usage" : { "accountEmail" : "<redacted>", "primary" : { "usedPercent" : 100 }, "secondary" : { "usedPercent" : 0 }, "tertiary" : { "usedPercent" : 0 } } }
]
```

Behavior preservation note: with my normal config containing an Antigravity token account, `--source cli` returned OAuth because selected token accounts intentionally route through `[oauth]`; the temporary no-token-account config above verifies the new `local -> cli` fallback path without exercising OAuth credentials.
target.author: enieuwy
